title: Sequential decision and reinforcement concepts deterministic AI draft bank

publication.changeSummary: Misconception MCQ, evidence-boundary fill-gap for Sequential decision and reinforcement concepts; all items remain blocked from formal exams pending independent human review.

items.title: Sequential decision and reinforcement concepts: reject the overclaim
items.prompt: Which statement is source-bounded and corrects this AI misconception? A reinforcement-learning agent discovers the true objective automatically from experience, so reward design is only a technical detail.

examEligibility.rationale: AI-assisted unreviewed draft; independent human technical, source, pedagogy, safety, accessibility, and answer-oracle review is required before formal-exam eligibility.

hints: Choose the statement that names mechanism, evidence conditions, and the boundary of the claim rather than a fluent or benchmark-based overgeneralization.

feedback.correct: Correct. Treat reward as a proxy chosen by people, test reward-hacking and distribution shift, and enforce safety or rights constraints outside a single optimized score.
feedback.incorrect: That option preserves the overclaim. Treat reward as a proxy chosen by people, test reward-hacking and distribution shift, and enforce safety or rights constraints outside a single optimized score.

criteria.description: Selects the source-bounded correction and rejects the documented AI overclaim.

privateAuthorNotes: Human reviewer must verify the correction against cited sources and ensure the distractor does not normalize unsafe or overstated claims.

options.text: Treat reward as a proxy chosen by people, test reward-hacking and distribution shift, and enforce safety or rights constraints outside a single optimized score.

items.title: Sequential decision and reinforcement concepts: canonical evidence boundary

hints: Retrieve the short checkpoint from the correction and ensure it narrows rather than expands the evidence claim.

feedback.correct: Correct. The checkpoint is 'reward as a proxy'. Treat reward as a proxy chosen by people, test reward-hacking and distribution shift, and enforce safety or rights constraints outside a single optimized score.
feedback.incorrect: The phrase must preserve the technical and responsible-AI boundary. Treat reward as a proxy chosen by people, test reward-hacking and distribution shift, and enforce safety or rights constraints outside a single optimized score.

criteria.description: Supplies the exact canonical phrase that completes the bounded source-aligned correction.

privateAuthorNotes: Human reviewer must verify the accepted phrase and technically equivalent variants before learner use.
